label: Standard — neuroscience guest on productivity podcast

input.professional_role: Cognitive neuroscientist who studies attention and focus in digital environments. He has a new book arguing that the human attention span hasn't shortened — the environment has become more demanding.
input.show_topic: Productivity and work optimization podcast for knowledge workers
input.booking_reason: His argument directly contradicts the most common claim in productivity content (that attention spans are shrinking), which makes for a compelling counter-narrative episode
input.recent_work: His book 'The Attention Myth' published 3 months ago; a widely-shared article in a science magazine arguing against the 'goldfish attention span' claim

input.professional_role: Veteran restaurant owner who has opened and closed four restaurants over 20 years and now consults other restaurateurs on sustainable business models
input.show_topic: A podcast about small business and entrepreneurship
input.booking_reason: Her experience with both success and failure makes her a more honest voice than the typical success-story guest

label: European context — EU energy policy expert

input.professional_role: Energy policy researcher at a German think tank specializing in the European energy transition. She focuses on the political economy of renewable energy deployment across EU member states.
input.show_topic: European affairs podcast covering policy, politics, and the future of the EU
input.booking_reason: The EU is about to announce its updated 2035 energy targets, and she is one of the most cited researchers on whether those targets are achievable given current political constraints
input.recent_work: A policy paper titled 'The 2035 Gap: Why Europe's Energy Targets Don't Match Its Political Reality', published by a major Berlin-based think tank; a panel appearance at the Brussels Energy Forum last month
input.topics_to_avoid: She has requested not to discuss her previous role advising a specific national government

input.professional_role: Former pharmaceutical executive turned whistleblower. He exposed pricing practices at his former company and was subsequently fired and sued. He won the lawsuit but lost his marriage and career. Now writes and speaks about corporate ethics.
input.show_topic: Business ethics podcast for MBA students and corporate professionals
input.booking_reason: He can speak from both sides — he participated in the system he later exposed, which makes his perspective more credible and more complicated than a simple hero narrative
input.recent_work: His book 'The Price of Speaking Up' published last month; testimony before a Senate committee
input.topics_to_avoid: The ongoing litigation with his former employer — his lawyers have specifically restricted what he can discuss. Also avoid personal life — the divorce is off limits.

input.professional_role: Co-founder of a Lagos-based agritech startup that uses satellite imagery and SMS-based alerts to help smallholder farmers in West Africa predict crop diseases. The company serves 800,000 farmers across Nigeria, Ghana, and Côte d'Ivoire.
input.show_topic: Emerging markets technology podcast focused on African innovation
input.booking_reason: His company solved the smartphone assumption problem — most agritech fails because it assumes smartphone access. His SMS-first approach works on $15 feature phones and has a 94% farmer retention rate.
input.recent_work: Series A funding of $12 million from a pan-African VC; partnership with the Nigerian Ministry of Agriculture
input.topics_to_avoid: Competitor comparisons — the space is small and relationships matter

label: High-profile guest with strict time constraint

input.professional_role: Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist and former foreign correspondent. Covered six wars over 30 years. Just published what she says will be her final book, about the psychological cost of conflict journalism.
input.show_topic: Journalism and media podcast for working journalists
input.booking_reason: She is the most decorated living war correspondent and her retirement book is generating massive industry interest
input.recent_work: Her book 'The Last Dispatch' published this week; a viral essay in a major magazine about why she stopped going to war zones
input.topics_to_avoid: She will not discuss specific intelligence sources. She will not comment on current military operations. She has limited patience for questions she considers naive about the realities of conflict reporting.
input.time_constraint: 15 minutes maximum — her publicist was very firm about this
